react.answer:
## Building an Accordion in React **Mental model:** An accordion is a list of header/panel pairs where opening a panel toggles its visibility. The only real state is *which ids are open* — model it as a `Set`. A single `allowMultiple` flag decides whether opening one closes the others. ### Step 1 — Track open ids in a Set A `Set` makes membership checks and toggling clean, and naturally supports multiple open panels. ```jsx const [openIds, setOpenIds] = useState(() => new Set()); const isOpen = (id) => openIds.has(id); ``` ### Step 2 — Toggle with single/multi behaviour Start from the existing set (multi) or an empty one (single), then add or remove the clicked id. ```jsx const toggle = (id) => setOpenIds((prev) => { const next = new Set(allowMultiple ? prev : []); prev.has(id) ? next.delete(id) : next.add(id); return next; }); ``` ### Step 3 — Render headers and conditional panels Each header is a ` {isOpen(item.id) &&
{item.content}
} ``` ### Step 4 — Show open state visually Rotate a chevron and tint the active header so the state is obvious at a glance. ```jsx ⌄ ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Duplicate ids | Caller's responsibility; `Set` dedupes silently. | | All collapsed | Valid state — empty `Set`. | | Switching to single mode | Next toggle resets to a one-item set. | **Interview tip:** Using a `Set` (not an array or a per-item boolean) is the detail that reads as senior — it makes both `allowMultiple` modes fall out of the same toggle function.

vue.answer:
## Building an Accordion in Vue 3 **Mental model:** An accordion is a list of header/panel pairs; the only state worth tracking is *which ids are open*. A `Set` held in a `ref` captures that cleanly, and an `allowMultiple` prop decides whether opening one panel closes the rest. ### Step 1 — Hold open ids in a reactive Set Use a `ref` wrapping a `Set` and a small helper for membership. ```vue ``` ### Step 2 — Toggle with single/multi behaviour Reassign a new `Set` so Vue's reactivity picks up the change. ```js function toggle(id) { const next = new Set(props.allowMultiple ? openIds.value : []); openIds.value.has(id) ? next.delete(id) : next.add(id); openIds.value = next; } ``` ### Step 3 — Render with v-for and v-if Each header is a `
{{ item.content }}
``` ### Step 4 — Reflect state in the UI Bind a class to rotate the chevron and tint the open header. ```vue ⌄ ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | All collapsed | Valid — empty `Set`. | | Reassign vs mutate | Always reassign `openIds.value` so reactivity fires. | | Single mode | Each toggle starts from an empty set. | **Interview tip:** Mention *why* you reassign the `Set` instead of calling `.add()` in place — Vue 3 tracks the `ref`'s value identity, so an in-place mutation of the same `Set` wouldn't reliably trigger updates.

angular.answer:
## Building an Accordion in Angular **Mental model:** Render header/panel pairs with `*ngFor`, and keep a private `Set` of open ids on the component. An `allowMultiple` input decides whether opening one panel collapses the others. Classic `NgModule` component — no signals or standalone APIs, and no `?.`/`??` (the build rejects them). ### Step 1 — Track open ids Hold a private `Set` and expose an `isOpen` helper for the template. ```typescript private openIds = new Set(); isOpen(id: string): boolean { return this.openIds.has(id); } ``` ### Step 2 — Toggle with single/multi behaviour Clear the set first in single mode, then add or remove the clicked id. ```typescript toggle(id: string): void { const wasOpen = this.openIds.has(id); if (!this.allowMultiple) { this.openIds.clear(); } if (wasOpen) { this.openIds.delete(id); } else { this.openIds.add(id); } } ``` ### Step 3 — Render headers and panels Use a `
{{ item.content }}
``` ### Step 4 — Reflect state with style bindings A ternary `[style.transform]` rotates the chevron; another tints the header. ```html ⌄ ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | All collapsed | Valid — empty `Set`. | | Single mode | `clear()` before the add keeps only one open. | | Missing aria | `[attr.aria-expanded]` sets the real attribute (not a DOM property). | **Interview tip:** Use `[attr.aria-expanded]`, not `[aria-expanded]` — `aria-*` are attributes, not properties, so the `attr.` prefix is what actually writes them to the DOM.
